title: No documentation on system event type
body:
**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

`system` event type is not documented in https://github.com/containers/libpod/blob/master/docs/podman-events.1.md even though current podman sends these events through varlink API.

Would it be also possible to explain when some events are delivered? (I am currently trying to find out when `system` event with `status = refresh` is sent.


**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:            1.5.1
RemoteAPI Version:  1
Go Version:         go1.12.7
OS/Arch:            linux/amd64
```

comments.body: `podman system refresh` is done when Podman detects a system reboot and resets the internal state. We should document this in the manpages, though.

Thank you for documenting event types! I have some additional questions. All of these are related to [cockpit](https://github.com/cockpit-project/cockpit-podman) plugin. Sometimes happens that we stop getting new events from varlink. There has been a few reports that all seem to affect this, I just thought if this could be related as well, since we see a few `system` events as well and we do not process them  yet.

> `podman system refresh` is done when Podman detects a system reboot and resets the internal state. 

What would it mean in practice. Does it mean that when we have open varlink connect that this connection would not be useful anymore and we need to get a new connection? Would it mean that we need to re-read all images and containers? Or it just means that it updated something in its internal state and nothing on the outside has changed? Also I struggle with ` Podman detects a system reboot` - since I see this just in random times. Is system reboot something different than I thing of it in this context?
Also can I trigger this somehow to conduct some experiments? 
```
$ podman system refresh
Error: unrecognized command `podman system refresh`
```

Also similar questions for `renumber`, except that I see how I can trigger it `podman system renumber`

For `refresh`:
This is an internal-only function that, on detecting a reboot, resets Podman's internal state (to account for the fact that everything is now unmounted, no containers are running, etc). It cannot be triggered by command, but instead happens automatically the first time a Podman command is run after reboot.

If you're seeing it at seemingly random intervals, then something is seriously wrong, and I'd strongly consider filing a bug. It should happen only once per reboot, on the very first command. Cockpit should never see it if you're only streaming events, as the Podman process would trigger it before it started serving events. We mostly use the event notification for debugging issues with reboot detection.

`system renumber` means that Podman has begun an internal lock reallocation. All containers and pods will be brought down (you'll still get separate events for this), allocated a fresh lock, and then restarted (if they were running). I do not anticipate this affecting `podman events` output significantly, so it should be safe to keep running through a renumber.
